text: RERA stands for Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act. It is a regulation that aims to protect the interests of homebuyers and promote transparency in the real estate sector. According to the information provided, Housing.com does not verify or endorse compliance of a specific project with RERA regulations, and users should verify all details independently from the REPs or from the RERA website before making any decision in relation to the purchase of properties [chunk_id: housing_delhi_052_001_chunk_013 | url: https://careers.housing.com/terms-of-use/].

Where can i buy a house in delhi under 50 lakhs

In Delhi, you can consider the following areas for buying a house under Rs 50 lakhs:

1. Narela: EWS flats are available in Narela, with prices starting from Rs 11.8 lakh to Rs 11.9 lakh [chunk_id: housing_delhi_004_002_chunk_005 | url: https://housing.com/news/apply-dda-housing-schemes/].
2. Rohini: LIG flats are available in Rohini Sector 34 and 35, with prices starting from Rs 14 lakh to Rs 14.2 lakh [chunk_id: housing_delhi_004_002_chunk_005 | url: https://housing.com/news/apply-dda-housing-schemes/].
3. Ramgarh Colony: LIG flats are available in Ramgarh Colony, with prices starting from Rs 13.1 lakh to Rs 14.5 lakh [chunk_id: housing_delhi_004_002_chunk_005 | url: https://housing.com/news/apply-dda-housing-schemes/].

Please note that prices may vary depending on the location, size, and amenities of the property. It's always a good idea to research and visit the areas in person to get a better sense of the market and available options.
